tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer expressed strong negative sentiment about product limitations and experienced high effort due to unresolved reporting issues. The AI Agent provided low-quality responses, and the teammate did not resolve the issue.
tickets.body:
Good Morning,    If whoever is reading this email is unable to help with my request, please escalate to a manager or senior tech.    We have a financial manager that collaborates with another financial team at our organization. He doesn’t send docs for signature but needs to look at them but this is not possible without Admin rights which we don’t want to give out to someone who is not an admin.    I am an admin and can see the reports but SignNow has SO many limitations. Here are a few:      In document reports, it allows you to pick a data range for a year (for example), but it doesn’t display or export a years’ worth of data.  
 You can’t search for multiple criteria in any filter section except for the status. 
 I searched for document names beginning with FY250 but it showed no results. 
    So I connected with support, Lena told me to let her know what I needed and it sounded like she could run the report for me but what she sent wasn’t anything remotely close to what I had asked. I’m told these are the limitations of SignNow but I’m pretty sure there must be some way on your end to be able to query the actual database and provide us with this report so that we can do what we need to for our business without spending all day long running reports a month at a time. By the way it looks on my end, even if we did that, we would not be able find any documents that begin with FY250.    I can’t imagine a company like yours would just leave us hanging like this.     This is what we are looking for:     We want a report that starts on March 1, 2025 until today and filter for Document Names that start with FY250 & FY260 (the filter doesn’t let you use more than 1 criteria per filter section which is another big limitation).   We only need to see the statuses Pending and Complete.   We would like a report like this every 2 weeks after that for just these records since the last report but if this part is not possible at least one good report would help.    Please do the right thing, get me in tough with a real manager and let’s get this thing resolved.      Nelson Pidgeon       Project Coordinator, Web     Boston Public Health Commission     617-534-2854 (office)     617-275-1764 (cell)      npidgeon@bphc.org
tickets.subject: Need additional support

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer positively values the signNow platform but is frustrated by the account suspension under Section 5.2 of the Terms of Service. Despite providing detailed verification and explanations, the teammate upheld the suspension, leaving the issue unresolved.
tickets.body:
Dear signNow Support Team,  
  I am writing to appeal the suspension of my account associated with the email address   vvsolod@gmail.com              , which was flagged for a Section 5.2 Terms of Service violation. Additionally, I am currently unable to log into my other account,   slava@nansen.id              .       
 I recently switched to signNow from DocuSign and have been thoroughly enjoying your platform. However, I believe my account was mistakenly flagged by your automated anti-spam/anti-fraud system. 
 Here is the context of the activity that likely triggered the suspension: 
 
   I was sending a legitimate personal legal document titled "20260528 Acknowledgement of Outstanding Obligations"             to a group of my actual creditors.        
   To streamline the process, I sent the signing request to my own email address and placed the recipients in the CC field            .        
   The cover message contained external Google Drive links providing proof of my assets (collateral) for these obligations            .        
   The document naturally contained financial terminology and amounts            .        
 
 I understand that sending a document to myself with multiple people in CC and including external links can look like a phishing or spam attempt to an automated bot. However, I assure you this was a genuine, targeted communication to people I know personally. 
  Could you please have a human reviewer look into this false positive and restore access to my accounts (  vvsolod@gmail.com               and   slava@nansen.id              )?       
 Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to continuing to use signNow. 
 Best regards, 
 Vladislav (Slava) Solodkiy 
 
  
 Fri, 29 May 2026 07:25, signNow < mail@signnow.com >:

At a later time, we want the ability to trigger signNow to send an email invitation to only selected signers, without changing the already issued embedded link(s) (i.e., keep the same signing URL that the signer has).    Ideally, this “send email later” action should be possible per signer (not to all recipients at once), and should not require re-creating the invite.             Question:    Is it technically possible to start with embedded signing (link delivery) and later send signNow email invitations for specific signers without regenerating / changing the signing link?          Thank you.            Kind regards,     Michos Ioannis

tickets.body: Hi,  We have a user within our SignNow  umich.org  that noticed a change in their SignNow experience this past Tuesday 4/28 which is causing them considerable confusion.. The issue they reported is       When I upload and send documents from the shared team folder, the completed documents are automatically saving under “Owned by Me” instead of remaining in the shared folder for the team.      This is a change from our normal workflow, as documents previously stayed in the shared folder so all team members could track sent and signed items.              They tell us they reached out directly to SignNow and were told-   this is controlled by a workspace-level setting:   “Directory where to save document copies sent from Team space.”   SignNow advised updating this setting to  Team Space/public (instead of personal)  so that sent documents remain in the shared team folder.        We "University of Michigan" have not completely switched over to workspaces for our org's, so I don't believe this 'fix' applies to our users. From my understanding from my team and their conversations with our representative Arti Harchekar, we do not have the admin aspects of workspaces yet.       Is it possible something was changed on the backside of this team    O & P Missing Doc and DM Footwear    or the team owner   chambest@umich.edu  , that you can change back?  Thank you.    David Sulick University of Michigan Business Systems Analyst Donor & Alumni Relationship Tool (DART), Document Imaging, E-Signature, Mcard & digital Asset Collaborative (DAC) Information and Technology Services

( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   If you are seeing an error while opening or signing, try Incognito/Private mode and clear cache/cookies, and note your browser and version. tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer experienced a negative product issue with document routing changes disrupting workflow. The AI Agent provided high-quality, clear guidance, but the customer faced high effort due to access limitations and role confusion.
tickets.body:
so what we did was not working in my prior ticket, but I wanted to make sure you understood what is happenning and who I need to reach out to fix this issue. Thank you again for your help; I want to explain the issue in more detail to make sure we’re aligned. Our workflow is the following: We go into our Team Shared Folder: “O & P Missing Doc and DM Footwear” We select a subfolder (for example: “Sent O&D/Waiver”) We click “Create” --> “Upload Documents” (this shows: “Upload or Create and add to ‘O & P Missing Doc and DM Footwear’ team”) We upload the document and send it to the patient Before Tuesday: The document would remain in the same shared folder we were working in This allowed our team to track all sent and completed documents in one place Now (starting Tuesday): After sending the document, it is automatically routed to our personal “Owned by Me” documents (Outbox) We now have to manually move each document back into the shared folder Important detail: This behavior is not consistent across users The Team Owner can follow the exact same steps, and their documents still remain in the shared folder. However, for myself and another team member (Avery) (who are "Admins" in the Team Group, the documents are routing to our personal folders instead. Additional note: Avery and I are able to move each other’s documents within the shared folders The Team Owner is not able to move our documents (which has always been the case) Because of this, it seems like documents are being created with different ownership depending on the user, even though we are all working within the same team and folder. This behavior changed suddenly on Tuesday without any changes on our end 
  ​
tickets.account: The Reqents of the University of Michigan

I have the full audit log confirming continued usage under this account.  I need the following resolved immediately:  1.My admin login :  Spiros.C@LexingtonHL.net  and our registered company main account is - ( SignNOW.001@LexingtonHL.net ) properly linked to our paid company subscription so API access reflects our active plan. 2.Confirmation of our current subscription tier, renewal date, and the API entitlements included. 3.Direct contact information for our assigned Account Executive or Account Manager.  I have attempted to reach support by phone at 1-800-831-2050 (the number appears to be disconnected), through your online support portal, and via prior emails — none of which have resulted in a response.  Please treat this as urgent. We are a mortgage lending company (NMLS# 347811) and depend on this platform for legally binding e-signature workflows with borrowers. tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer requested refunds for three unauthorized renewals citing French and EU law. The teammate refunded only the most recent renewal citing internal policy, which the customer disputed, escalating the issue to the legal team. The customer showed persistence and dissatisfaction with the refund policy.
tickets.body: Dear SignNow Support,    I am writing to request an immediate and full refund of three unauthorized automatic renewals that were charged to my PayPal account on May 17th of 2024, 2025 and 2026, for a total amount of 288.00 USD.     PayPal Transaction details:     - 2024 (May 17, 2024): Transaction ID 1D555416D4852783Y – 96.00 USD  - 2025 (May 17, 2025): Transaction ID 637832770M3144235 – 96.00 USD  - 2026 (May 17, 2026): Transaction ID 3YY62766W3631471K – 96.00 USD     Total claimed: 288.00 USD     Important facts:     - I have never actively used your services, neither in 2024, nor in 2025, nor in 2026.  - I never received any prior renewal notification (as required by French consumer law, Article L.215-1, and the EU Directive 2011/83/EU) for any of these three annual renewals. I only received invoices after each payment.  - Therefore, none of these three automatic renewals were properly authorized. My consent was not informed and is considered invalid under European and French law.     As a result, I am entitled to a full refund of the last three renewal payments (2024, 2025 and 2026), according to the principle of répétition de l'indu (Article 1302-1 of the French Civil Code) and the 5-year statute of limitations (Article 2224).     What I need from you:     1. Full refund of 288.00 USD (96 USD × 3) to my PayPal account.  2. tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer reported email deliverability issues with signature invites being flagged as spam or malicious and sought technical details about sender authentication and domain options. The AI Agent provided content-related advice but deferred technical questions to human support, and the customer followed up to check on request status.
tickets.body:
Hi SignNow Support,  We're a SignNow customer (Rock Rabbit) reaching out about an email deliverability issue we're seeing on outbound signature invites sent from  mail@signnow.com .  Summary Over the past week, three of our customers have independently reported that signature invite emails originating from  mail@signnow.com  are landing in their spam folders, and in at least one case the message was flagged by the recipient's email provider as "malicious." These are legitimate invites tied to documents our team sent through SignNow — not bulk or marketing email.  Because  mail@signnow.com  is a sender domain you own and control, there's nothing we can change on our DNS side (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) to address this. We're hoping you can help us understand what's going on and what options are available.  Specific questions 1. Is  mail@signnow.com  sent from a shared sending pool across SignNow customers? If so, have you seen recent reputation issues with the IPs or domain that pool uses — particularly with Microsoft / Outlook.com / Office 365, since the "marked as malicious" framing usually points to Microsoft's SmartScreen filter?  2. Do you offer a way to send signature invites from a customer-controlled subdomain (e.g.,  sign.rockrabbit.ai ) where we'd publish our own SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records and inherit our own sender reputation? Some eSignature providers offer this as "branded sending" or a "custom send-as domain."  3. If a shared pool is the only option, can you confirm the current SPF, DKIM, and DMARC posture for  mail@signnow.com , and whether there's a customer-side configuration we can use to improve deliverability (e.g., a different sender display name, reply-to setting, or branded subdomain on a higher tier)?  4. Are you currently aware of, or actively investigating, deliverability complaints from other customers in the past 1–2 weeks?  Account / impact - Account: Rock Rabbit (signing under our SignNow workspace) - Sender address recipients saw:  mail@signnow.com  - Reported by: 3 separate customers in the past week - Reported behavior: emails routed to spam; at least one inbox surfaced a "malicious" warning rather than a plain spam label - We can share message-IDs, recipient domains, and screenshots if helpful — let us know the best secure channel.  For context, I've already reviewed your KB article on preventing emails from going to spam ( https://www.signnow.com/help/prevent-emails-spam ). That guide is focused on content-side levers (subject lines, trigger words, whitelisting requests), which we'll continue to follow, but it doesn't address sender authentication or reputation — which is what we believe is actually driving these flags. 
   
 Thanks,  
 Glenn  
   
 --          
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
       
     rockrabbit.ai   
 
 
  Glenn Billman  
  Manager, Building Decarbonization & Operations  
 
 
 
   
 
  (650) 283 0124   
   glenn.billman@rockrabbit.ai
tickets.subject: Emails flagged as malicious

tickets.body: Hello,  Thank you for the update regarding the citation matter.  To clarify, I did not expect work to begin before payment was completed, so I was a bit surprised to see progress already taking place.  I also wanted to ask whether any attempt has been made, or is planned, to delay or continue the court date in order to potentially improve the chances of a better outcome on the case, rather than proceeding on the current date.  Additionally, based on your experience, what would you estimate the likelihood of a successful outcome to be in this situation?  Lastly, while we are not making any decisions at this time, I would also like to understand what the next steps would be if we decided not to move forward with representation.  Thank you in advance for the clarification.        Best regards  Abdalla Ibrahim

Hello Team,          We are currently facing an issue while sending document invites using the SignNow API from the LegalSignNowProxy user.    Problem Description:    We are unable to send invites that include a custom subject and custom message when using the POST API. We are receiving the following error:          "message": "Upgrade your subscription plan to personalize invite subject and message."          Please find the attached screenshot for reference.    However, the same functionality works correctly when sending invites through the SignNow UI, where both the custom subject and message are successfully delivered to recipients.          This indicates that the feature is available, but it may not be functioning as expected via the API or there may be a discrepancy in plan enforcement between UI and API.          Impact:    This issue is affecting our automated workflows, as we rely on the API to send document invites with personalized communication.          Please let us know if you need any additional details, such as request payloads, API logs, or error responses.    Looking forward to your support in resolving this issue.           
       Thanks & Regards, 
        ||    Sumeet Singh       Software Engineer, Global Security and Legal Tech       Lowe’s Companies Inc.             NOTICE: All information in and attached to the e-mails below may be proprietary, confidential, privileged and otherwise protected from improper or erroneous disclosure. If you are not the sender's intended recipient, you are not authorized to intercept, read, print, retain, copy, forward, or disseminate this message. If you have erroneously received this communication, please notify the sender immediately by phone (704-758-1000) or by e-mail and destroy all copies of this message electronic, paper, or otherwise. By transmitting documents via this email: Users, Customers, Suppliers and Vendors collectively acknowledge and agree the transmittal of information via email is voluntary, is offered as a convenience, and is not a secured method of communication; Not to transmit any payment information E.G. credit card, debit card, checking account, wire transfer information, passwords, or sensitive and personal information E.G. Driver's license, DOB, social security, or any other information the user wishes to remain confidential; To transmit only non-confidential information such as plans, pictures and drawings and to assume all risk and liability for and indemnify Lowe's from any claims, losses or damages that may arise from the transmittal of documents or including non-confidential information in the body of an email transmittal. Thank you.

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer experienced frustration with the product's billing design and company policies due to lack of clear disclosure and rigid prepayment blocks. The customer showed high effort by persistently seeking clarifications and escalation. The AI Agent provided low-quality responses, while the teammate delivered high-quality, detailed answers that ultimately resolved the billing concerns through migration to a usage-based model.
tickets.body:
Subject: Request for full plan terms disclosure — Account ID 15628117 
 Hello SignNow Support, 
 I am writing regarding my Business Annually subscription (Account ID: 15628117,  primelinea@gmail.com , invoice 0198b384-8a81-70cf-8897-99da70bb3fc0 dated 08/16/2025). 
 I have used 94 signature invites so far. My account dashboard indicates that a charge of €96 will be applied after 106 invites, which appears to be an overage / auto-renewal trigger I was not clearly informed about at the time of purchase. 
 I have three concerns: 
 1) The 100-invite-per-user limit and the €0.96 per-invite overage    rate are NOT disclosed on your public pricing page    (signnow.com/features/signnow-pricing). They are only referenced    indirectly in Terms of Service §8.2 as "units (for example    documents or transactions)", which a reasonable customer would    not understand as referring to signature invites. 
 2) The "Business Annually" plan is described in marketing as    "$8/user/month billed annually", with no mention of an invite    quota. This is a material term that affects the purchase decision    and, in my view, should be prominently disclosed. 
 3) The upcoming €96 charge will trigger before my annual renewal    date (08/16/2026), without an explicit consent step from me at    that moment. 
 I would like to request the following: 
 a) A written confirmation of the full terms of my specific plan:    - Exact invite quota per period    - Exact overage rate    - When and how auto-renewal / overage charges are triggered    - Cancellation policy and refund eligibility 
 b) Given the lack of clear upfront disclosure, I would appreciate    either (i) an increase of my invite quota for the current period    at no extra cost, or (ii) a waiver of the upcoming €96 overage    charge as a one-time goodwill gesture. 
 c) If neither (a) nor (b) is acceptable, please confirm in writing    that I may cancel auto-renewal immediately while retaining access    to the service until the paid-through date of 08/16/2026, with    no further charges. 
  ​

Please add 2 additional licenses to account, when you are able to do so.      To access your owner's portal, please visit  www.ghertner.com/owner .   For the fastest service, please submit all requests (including pool key, maintenance and architectural) and make payments through the owner portal. You may also email your requests directly to    hsr@ghertner.com   .
tickets.subject: Additional licenses required

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer sought a refund for a subscription purchased via Google Play. The AI Agent provided clear guidance and acknowledged the refund request details, facilitating a smooth resolution process.
tickets.body:
---------- Forwarded message --------- From:  < googleplay-support@google.com >  Date: Mon, May 11, 2026, 9:40 AM Subject: Your Google Support Inquiry : [9-0924000041030] To: < gracekatie155@gmail.com > 
 
   
     Hi  Kathleen , 
 Thank you for reaching out to Google Play Support.  My name is Nova and I appreciate you for reaching out to us. I am writing to provide you with an update regarding your refund request. 
 I have carefully reviewed your request, and I sincerely wish I had better news to share. Unfortunately, the refund has been  declined  as it does not meet the criteria of Google Play’s current policies. I truly understand how disappointing this is, especially when you are looking for a swift resolution. 
  Next Steps with the Developer  To ensure you still have a path toward a resolution, I recommend contacting the app developer directly at   support@signnow.com  . As the creators of the game, they have the authority to review individual cases for their players and can often provide solutions that fall outside of our standard refund windows. 
 
 
 Response Time:  They typically respond within  3 business days . 
 
 Follow-up:  If you do not hear back from them after that time, please reach back out to us with a screenshot of your sent email, and we will be happy to look into the matter further. 
 
  Securing Your Account  To help prevent any accidental or unauthorized purchases, I highly recommend setting up  password or PIN protection  on your device. This adds a layer of security to your account and requires a password to complete any transaction.

tickets.body:
Hello,    I’m investigating an issue where documents opened through the SignNow embedded editor are showing in View Only mode with no field/editor menu available. This is happening in both our production and staging environments.    Below is a clean staging reproduction.    I uploaded a brand-new PDF in our application, then used our SignNow integration to create an embedded editor link. SignNow successfully created the document and returned an editor link, but opening that link showed the document in View Only mode.    I also opened the same document directly from the SignNow dashboard while signed in as the document owner, and it still showed View Only with no editor/field menu.    Staging document details:      SignNow document id:  49113734b2ca4147b82490375397f4b2ddee94a8 
 
  Document owner shown in SignNow metadata:   tfritz@torticity.com  
 
  Internal tracking id in our system:  1773 
 
  Source document id in our system:  26924 
 
  Lead/case id in our system:  4805 
 
    The SignNow document metadata appears to rule out the documented View Only causes:       field_invites: [] 
 
   signatures: [] 
 
   roles: [] 
 
   requests: [] 
 
   document_group_info: null 
 
   has_fields: false 
 
   original_owner: true 
 
    So the document does not appear to have been sent, invited, signed, or added to a document group, and it appears to be owned by the API user.    Can you help confirm why this document is opening in View Only mode? I asked for API support within the chatbot, but have not received an answer as of yet.               Nicole Garofalo     Software Engineer          ngarofalo@torticity.com        
      WARNING:  CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE - The information enclosed with this transmission is the private, confidential property of the sender, and the material is privileged communication intended solely for the individual indicated. If you are not the intended recipient, you are notified that any review, disclosure, copying, distribution, or the taking of any other action relevant to the contents of this transmission are strictly prohibited. If you have received this transmission in error, please notify me immediately at     ngarofalo@torticity.com

Hello                 I trust this email finds you in good spirits.  We have contributing guest posts to our platform,  https://theusaleaders.com/ .  We are thrilled to inform you that we are keen to move forward with your submission. To proceed, we kindly request a draft or post from your end. This will allow us to review it internally with our editorial team for approval. Upon approval, we will promptly publish it on our website and share the live link with you.  For your reference, please find the pricing details below:   General Post:  $300 USD per post (Maximum of 2 do-follow links allowed per post)  Casinos/Crypto/Gambling/  CBD: $600 USD per post    Understanding that your objectives may extend beyond   Theusaleader.com   we are pleased to offer you access to other high-traffic websites where we can publish your articles at the same rates:       No.      Website Name      Website URL      DA       1    Mirror Review     https://www.mirrorreview.com     64      2    Business APAC     https://www.businessapac.com/     58      3    The Education Magazine     https://www.theeducationmagazine.com/     61      4    The USA Leaders     https://theusaleaders.com/     52              Important point to notice   :    1.   Immediate payment after publishing the article.  (we will wait for 3 days and after that your link will be removed.)    2.   Link will be active for 1 year   (we don’t do permanent posting on our website.    As we look to maintain consistency and quality across all guest articles published on our platform, I’ve compiled a set of clear guidelines for contributors. These guidelines will help you communicate our expectations effectively when discussing guest article opportunities with potential writers or partners.            Each post can include a maximum of  two do-follow links . Clearly specify which links are do-follow.    The same do-follow link cannot be repeated within a single post. We require different links.    Include one featured image, if necessary. A maximum of two additional sub-images can be added.    Links will remain active for one year.  We do not offer permanent link placements  on our website.    Immediate payment is required after the article is published. If payment is not made within three days, your link will be removed.    If the client delays payment, an additional charge will be applied for the same article.    Submissions should be between  [800-1500 words] .  We're experiencing a bug with our Team Documents folder in our Big Little Gyms workspace.

 Workspace details: - Workspace name: Big Little Gyms - Team: BLG Sales Team - 6 members

tickets.body:
Hey Lynne, support team, hope you're doing well!  
 
   
 We're a SignNow enterprise customer and looking to understand if there's a way to implement something a little bit dynamic with the signature fields. 
 
   
 I have a template in SignNow that looks like this: 
 
   
 
  
 
   
  
 It's got a mix of signature/date fields for the user to  sign, then some  smart text fields  that  I populate with  the  /integration/object/smartfields endpoint, and even some pre-filled data  that I'm populating  with  the / prefill -texts endpoint.     
  
   
 Specifically, the issue I'm running into is with populating this section below, which needs a signature ONLY in specific cases - if the user lives in Wisconsin. 
 
   
 
  
 
   
 
 Question:  how can I dynamically show a signature field? I know that there is an "Add Conditional Rule" on signature fields, but I want the Conditional Rule to depend on data like the Pre-Filled Text Fields from the API, not data that the user enters in during the signature process. 
 
   
 d  
 
 
   
 Please let me know if that makes sense. Thank you so much for any help.
tickets.subject: Specific question on SignNow signature fields

Hello,   I am writing regarding a charge of  $96 for an annual subscription  to signNow that was processed on my account without my authorization.   I do not actively use your platform and have not used this service for over two years. Additionally, I did not consent to any automatic renewal, nor was I aware of any active subscription tied to my account.   Given these facts, I find this charge unacceptable.   I am formally requesting the following:   
 
 Immediate cancellation  of this subscription and any associated auto-renewal. 
 
 Full refund of the $96 charge  processed without my authorization. 
 Confirmation in writing that my account has no active subscriptions moving forward. 
   You may verify within your system that my account has not been in use for a prolonged period, which further supports that this renewal was not knowingly authorized.   If this matter is not resolved promptly, I will proceed to dispute the charge with my financial institution and take further action as necessary.   I expect your prompt attention to this matter and a resolution without delay.    Kemery Montes
